expectations.text: A criterion benchmark file is produced (contains criterion_group! and criterion_main! macros)
The benchmark generates realistic test data at production scale (~2000 items, not toy sizes like 10)
expectations.evidence: generate_realistic_pagos(2000) is used for the main comparison group. Scaling benchmark tests 500/1000/2000/5000.

expectations.text: At least 2 alternative implementations are written as separate functions
expectations.evidence: alternatives.rs contains 3 alternatives: ingresos_numeric_key, ingresos_sort_scan, ingresos_preallocated, plus the original baseline.

expectations.text: All approaches are benchmarked in the same benchmark group for direct comparison
expectations.evidence: bench_comparison function uses a single benchmark group 'dashboard_aggregation' with all 4 approaches benchmarked via bench_function/bench_with_input.

expectations.text: Data generation happens OUTSIDE the benchmark loop (not inside b.iter())
expectations.evidence: Data is generated once: `let pagos = generate_realistic_pagos(2000);` before the group, then passed by reference inside `b.iter(|| ingresos_current(black_box(&pagos)))`.

expectations.text: The output includes a decision/recommendation based on expected benchmark results, not just theory
expectations.evidence: report.md includes 'Expected Results & Recommendation' section recommending ingresos_preallocated with specific expected gains (20-45%) and rationale tied to bottleneck analysis.
